title: Writing
text:
Science communication, feature writing, and public-facing work.




CO 301B · August 2025


Seeing Cities Clearly

Feature Article Urban Planning GIS AI

A feature article exploring how AI-powered drone remote sensing and machine learning are transforming urban planning and disaster resilience. Examines real-world applications in land-use classification, urban forest management, historic preservation, and disaster risk mapping.

Read (PDF)





CO 301B · July 2025


Concrete Jungles

Advance Article Urban Ecology Environmental Justice

An advance article on Schell et al. (2020) examining how systemic racism and economic inequality shape the ecological health of American cities, from biodiversity and air quality to climate resilience, and what justice-centered urban planning can do about it.

Read (PDF)

title: Zachary Cramton
text:
Zachary Cramton
Urban Planning · Environmental Science · Spatial Data

LinkedIn GitHub Email


Beaver Complex - Fourmile Creek, Fairplay, Colorado ©2026 Zachary Cramton





I’m an urban and environmental planner based in Fort Collins, Colorado, with a B.S. in Ecosystem Science and Sustainability from Colorado State University. My work sits at the intersection of environmental planning, placemaking, and data analysis — building tools, writing policy, and translating technical findings into decisions that improve how communities live in their landscapes.
Recently I’ve led a web and records overhaul for CSU’s Alternative Transportation Fee Advisory Board, authored a micromobility impact report for the Association for Commuter Transportation, and developed a spatial screening tool for beaver conflict and restoration analysis in ArcGIS Pro. My capstone project piloted the migration of NASA GLOBE Observer’s Mosquito Habitat Mapper to the CitSci platform in Fort Collins.
I’m currently seeking entry-level roles in urban and environmental planning, environmental consulting, or GIS. Reach me at zachary@cramton.com.

title: Portfolio
text:
Photography

I’m an amateur photographer with an interest in macro, landscape, nature, and aerial drone photography along the Colorado Front Range. A dedicated gallery is in development.
